prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `7bb29440`.
codex_response:
Implemented the new ARC2 task package for `7bb29440` in [arc2/tasks/task_7bb29440](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7bb29440). The rule is: find the disconnected solid rectangle with the smallest total number of `4` and `6` cells, then output that rectangle as an exact crop. I used the `opus46` hint and discarded the `sonnet45` overlay summary; that mismatch is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7bb29440/notes.md). No new primitive was needed, so `arc2/dsl_ext.py` was unchanged.

The package files are [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7bb29440/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7bb29440/verifier.py), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7bb29440/generator.py), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_7bb29440/notes.md). The generator matches the task family by building multiple non-overlapping `1`-filled rectangles on a `0` background, inserting sparse `4`/`6` markers, and enforcing one unique minimum-marker rectangle as the output target.

Validation passed end to end: official examples `6/6`, generated examples `1000/1000`. The built artifacts are [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7bb29440/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7bb29440/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7bb29440/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7bb29440/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/7bb29440/previews/preview_03.png). I also spot-checked representative built examples directly from the generated task data for distribution sanity.
